task: B2: teach the posting engine the cash_advance source type so an approved advance posts a real, balanced, traceable journal entry (not just a deduction). Engine-only; the approve-path call is B5.
details: PostingSourceType + validation list gain 'cash_advance'; buildCashAdvanceLines modeled on buildBillPaymentLines.

DEBIT = B1-mapped cash_advance account via resolveAccountForCategory(opco,'cash_advance','cash_advance') (no hardcoding). CREDIT = optional credit_account_id passed by caller (B5), else company-default cash-like account (bill_payment fallback).

Posts only when cash_advance_requests.status='approved'; amount = requested_amount_cents. New error code ADVANCE_NOT_POSTING_ELIGIBLE (added to the markBatchFailed exclusion list like the other *_NOT_POSTING_ELIGIBLE codes).
Resolver TS union EXPENSE_CATEGORY_MAP_KIND_VALUES gains 'cash_advance' (B1 added it to the DB CHECK; the TS union lagged).

Balanced via the existing assertBalanced guard (unmodified); audit spine emitted centrally by postSourceTransaction (transaction_source_links) — no separate emit. cash_advance intentionally excluded from batch backfill.
Did NOT change invoice/bill/customer_payment/bill_payment behavior; credit_account_id is optional so existing callers are untouched. No schema change.

Unit test (mocked DB + resolvers) proves: balanced debit==credit>0, DEBIT=resolved cash_advance account, CREDIT=passed account when provided / default when omitted, source_id linked, refuses non-approved.
